remember: Students will be able to recall and write the definition of a solution and its types.
Students will be able to identify and name the components of a solution.
Students will be able to recall and explain the concept of concentration of a solution.
understand: Students will be able to understand the concept of mole fraction and its relationship with other concentration units.
Students will be able to explain the law of definite proportions and its implications on solutions.
Students will be able to understand the concept of colligative properties and their dependence on the concentration of the solution.
apply: Students will be able to calculate the mole fraction and other concentration units of a solution.
Students will be able to apply the law of definite proportions to solve problems related to solutions.
Students will be able to calculate colligative properties such as relative lowering of vapour pressure, elevation in boiling point, and depression in freezing point of a solution.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the relationship between concentration and colligative properties of a solution.
Students will be able to compare and contrast different types of solutions and their properties.
Students will be able to evaluate the effect of concentration on the physical and chemical properties of a solution.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT objective 1: To understand the concept of a solution and its types.
To explain the concept of concentration and its units.
To understand the law of definite proportions and its implications on solutions.
To explain the concept of colligative properties and their dependence on the concentration of the solution.
board_exam_objectives: To define a solution and its types.
To explain the concept of concentration and its units.
To calculate the mole fraction and other concentration units of a solution.
To explain the law of definite proportions and its implications on solutions.
To calculate colligative properties such as relative lowering of vapour pressure, elevation in boiling point, and depression in freezing point of a solution.
